name: Egg Foo Yung- Shig
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the onion and slice it into 1/4 inch slices.
2. Slice the celery on the bias to make it 1/8-inch thick.
3. Rinse and drain the bean sprouts.
4. If the water chestnuts are large, halve them.
5. In a heavy skillet, sauté the onion and celery in a small amount of oil.
6. Add eggs, shrimp, bean sprouts, and water chestnuts to the skillet and mix well.
7. Rinse and dry the skillet. Heat 1/4 cup of oil in it.
8. Using a measuring cup, scoop out portions of the mixture and add them to the skillet to form patties (about four).
9. Cook the patties at medium heat until they are browned on one side.
10. Flip the patties and cook the other side until browned.
11. Remove the patties from the skillet and drain on paper towels.
12. Place the patties on a heated serving platter and keep warm until all are cooked.
13. To make the sauce, mix menmi, 8 tablespoons water, 1 teaspoon sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon soy sauce in a small saucepan.
14. Cook the mixture at medium heat until it boils, then reduce the heat to simmer.
15. Mix 1 tablespoon cornstarch with 2 tablespoons water and add it to the sauce, stirring to thicken.
16. Pour the sauce over the egg foo yung patties and serve.
